description:
Four different documents in the hand of Ḥalfon b. Menashshe (fl. 1100–1138 CE). Clockwise from upper left:

(1) Thin vertical strip from the left side of a document. Involves a certain Aharon and maybe Ḥassūn (l.2). Probably a partnership agreement dividing expenses and profits.

(2) Fragment (upper left piece) of a legal document. Involves: a woman's death; her mother and/or mother-in-law; Sitt al-Ahl; Abū l-Faraj Netanʾel; Ibn Qasāsa (this may be part of the name of Abū l-Faraj Netanʾel); a deceased Abū l-Faḍl. On verso is a list of names, quantities, and goods (such as rose water and flax), many of which are crossed out. This is likely an indirect join with BL OR 13153.4/10 (PGPID 19171).

(3) A person acknowledges that they have received all the rent due to them for an apartment in Alexandria. There are some interlinear corrections. 

(4) Draft containing formulaic legal phrases.
description_he: מסמך משפטי בAramaic, Hebrew, Judaeo-Arabic מהמאה ה-12. מוחזק בהספרייה הבריטית.
